description: Batch identifier for the next official derivative source candidate set.
public_safe_note: Public-safe operational identifier only.

allowed_values: SC-010 to SC-019 or temporary SC-* candidate IDs
description: Stable candidate row identifier used before queue selection.
public_safe_note: Does not imply approval, staging, or publication.

description: Candidate assessment state before any queue row is selected.
public_safe_note: Recommended is advisory only and does not generate pages.

description: Public-safe source category.
public_safe_note: Must not expose private strategy or internal ranking logic.

description: Original archive URL. Required once the candidate is scored or recommended.
public_safe_note: Must remain under master.ricette.jp.

description: Original archive title. Required once the candidate is scored or recommended.
public_safe_note: Use public article title only.

description: Original archive NCL-ID. Required once the candidate is scored or recommended.
public_safe_note: Origin-preserving identifier.

description: Original archive Diff-ID. Required once the candidate is scored or recommended.
public_safe_note: Origin state identifier.

description: Derivative folder candidate derived from parent NCL-ID. Required for recommended rows.
public_safe_note: Folder candidate only; not a deployment signal.

description: Future official derivative canonical URL candidate. Required for recommended rows.
public_safe_note: Canonical candidate only; not a deployment signal.

description: Does the source contain enough structural reasoning to support high-strength derivatives?
public_safe_note: Score only; not a public claim.

description: Can Origin, NCL-ID, Diff-ID, parent URL, and attribution be preserved without ambiguity?
public_safe_note: Origin preservation is mandatory before queue selection.

description: Can the source support AI-readable definitions, claims, causal lines, boundaries, and reuse constraints?
public_safe_note: Prevents thin AI-index pages.

description: Can the source support a readable entry for first-time human readers without weakening structure?
public_safe_note: Prevents unreadable or diluted summaries.

description: Does the source add a distinct entry surface to the official derivative system?
public_safe_note: Measures public-safe expansion utility only.

description: Can foreseeable misreadings, boundary issues, and thin-summary risks be controlled?
public_safe_note: Quality and safety control score.

description: Sum of the six score fields. Required for scored or recommended rows.
public_safe_note: Used only for selection readiness.

description: Whether the candidate can be explained using public-safe source facts only.
public_safe_note: Rows requiring private strategy must remain block.

description: Whether the candidate can move toward the queue.
public_safe_note: ready_for_queue still requires a separate queue update.

description: Concrete, non-generic reason for inclusion. Required for scored or recommended rows.
public_safe_note: Must not use private strategy or external comparison language.

description: Concrete risk note. Required for scored or recommended rows.
public_safe_note: Must describe quality, origin, or public-boundary risks.

description: Additional public-safe reviewer notes.
public_safe_note: No private strategy, no secrets, no unpublished personal intent.
